items.standfirst: The US president earlier questioned Meloni's popularity after suggesting she "begged" for a photo at G7 summit
items.content: The US president earlier questioned Meloni's popularity after suggesting she "begged" for a photo at G7 summit

items.title: Italy's Meloni criticizes Trump's "constant, unprovoked attacks"
items.standfirst: President Trump and Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni traded criticism on Saturday after Italy canceled its envoy's visit to the U.S.
items.content: President Trump and Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni traded criticism on Saturday after Italy canceled its envoy's visit to the U.S.

items.title: Trump says Italy's Meloni asked 'over and over' for a photo with him as diplomatic spat escalates
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump turned up the heat on a diplomatic row with Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni on Saturday, accusing her of repeatedly seeking a photograph with him at the G7 summit and blaming the clash between the two heads of state on tensions over Iran and NATO. Meloni later fired back, slamming the 'constant, unprovoked attacks' from Trump as 'senseless'.
items.content: US President Donald Trump turned up the heat on a diplomatic row with Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni on Saturday, accusing her of repeatedly seeking a photograph with him at the G7 summit and blaming the clash between the two heads of state on tensions over Iran and NATO. Meloni later fired back, slamming the 'constant, unprovoked attacks' from Trump as 'senseless'.

items.title: Trump-Meloni row intensifies as leaders trade barbs
items.standfirst: Italian prime minister Giorgia Meloni has called out US President Donald Trump for "senseless", "constant, unprovoked attacks", as a row triggered by his claim she begged him for a picture escalates.
items.content: Italian prime minister Giorgia Meloni has called out US President Donald Trump for "senseless", "constant, unprovoked attacks", as a row triggered by his claim she begged him for a picture escalates.

items.title: Trump ‘totally fabricated’ claim she begged him for a photo, Italy’s Meloni says
items.standfirst: After President Donald Trump said Giorgia Meloni had “begged” him for a photo at the G7 summit earlier this week, Italy’s Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni was quick to hit back.
items.content: After President Donald Trump said Giorgia Meloni had “begged” him for a photo at the G7 summit earlier this week, Italy’s Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni was quick to hit back.

items.title: 'Completely made up': Italy's PM on Trump's comments she begged him for photo
items.standfirst: Italian Prime ​Minister Giorgia Meloni has accused Donald Trump of making up a story about her, after ‌the US president told an Italian TV channel that she had "begged" him to take a picture with her at the G7 summit in France.
items.content: Italian Prime ​Minister Giorgia Meloni has accused Donald Trump of making up a story about her, after ‌the US president told an Italian TV channel that she had "begged" him to take a picture with her at the G7 summit in France.

ai_summary: Trump questions Meloni's popularity

items.title: Zelensky returns highest Polish honour after award stripped
items.standfirst: Ukraine's president said his country was open to "engagement" about "difficult and painful chapters of our shared past".
items.content: Ukraine's president said his country was open to "engagement" about "difficult and painful chapters of our shared past".

items.title: Zelensky returns Polish award as WWII row strains alliance
items.standfirst: Ukraine and Poland's close wartime partnership has been shaken by a growing dispute over World War II history, with President Volodymyr Zelensky and senior Ukrainian officials returning Polish state honours after Warsaw stripped him of its highest award.
items.content: Ukraine and Poland's close wartime partnership has been shaken by a growing dispute over World War II history, with President Volodymyr Zelensky and senior Ukrainian officials returning Polish state honours after Warsaw stripped him of its highest award.

items.title: Poland strips Zelensky of top state honour over WWII dispute
items.standfirst: Poland has withdrawn its highest state honour from Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, after Kyiv named an army unit after a nationalist group that massacred Polish civilians during World War II. The decision by President Karol Nawrocki threatens to unleash a serious diplomatic crisis between the two neighbours – just days before a major conference on Ukraine's reconstruction is due to be held in the Polish city of Gdańsk. Story by Charlotte Lam and Antoine Fenaux.
items.content: Poland has withdrawn its highest state honour from Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, after Kyiv named an army unit after a nationalist group that massacred Polish civilians during World War II. The decision by President Karol Nawrocki threatens to unleash a serious diplomatic crisis between the two neighbours – just days before a major conference on Ukraine's reconstruction is due to be held in the Polish city of Gdańsk. Story by Charlotte Lam and Antoine Fenaux.

items.title: Zelenskyy and top Ukrainian officials return Polish awards in WWII dispute
items.standfirst: The move comes after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy was stripped of Poland's top honour.
items.content: The move comes after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy was stripped of Poland's top honour.

items.title: Zelensky stripped of highest Polish honour over WW2 name of army unit
items.standfirst: Ukraine has denounced the move, calling it a "strategic mistake" and "disrespectful".
items.content: Ukraine has denounced the move, calling it a "strategic mistake" and "disrespectful".

ai_summary: Zelenskyy returns Polish honor after award stripped
Poland withdraws highest state honor from Zelenskyy

clusters.title: Uncertainty surrounds U.S.-Iran deal as Israel strikes Lebanon
clusters.neutral_headline: Israel and Hezbollah agree to ceasefire

items.title: Uncertainty surrounds U.S.-Iran deal as Israel strikes Lebanon
items.standfirst: Special envoy Steve Witkoff headed to Switzerland overnight to begin the postponed U.S.-Iran talks, as fighting continued in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah.
items.content: Special envoy Steve Witkoff headed to Switzerland overnight to begin the postponed U.S.-Iran talks, as fighting continued in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah.

items.title: Israel and Hezbollah renew ceasefire after deadly flareup disrupts opening of Iran talks
items.standfirst: At least five killed in Israeli air strike in southern Lebanon on Saturday morning, as US envoy Steve Witkoff reportedly prepares to open talks with Iranian foreign ministerWhat lessons will Iran’s new leadership draw from the 110-day war?‘It’s a big mistake’: Israelis feel betrayed and angry after Iran peace dealIsrael and Hezbollah agreed to renew a fragile ceasefire in Lebanon on Friday after 24 hours of intense violence that posed an early challenge to the new agreement between the US and Iran to end their conflict.A meeting that was scheduled to take place on Friday between Washington and Tehran in Switzerland to discuss implementation of the new deal was cancelled when Hezbollah killed four Israeli soldiers and Israel carried out a wave of retaliatory airstrikes in southern Lebanon and the Bekaa valley that killed at least 47 people. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.content: Israel and militant group Hezbollah agreed to a ceasefire Friday, after a deadly escalation between the two in Lebanon looked set to derail Washington-Tehran peace talks in Switzerland

clusters.title: Lebanese turtle conservationist Mona Khalil killed by Israeli strike

items.title: Lebanese turtle conservationist Mona Khalil killed by Israeli strike
items.standfirst: Mona Khalil, who had refused to leave the beach she had spent years protecting, died from her injuries after the Israeli strike.
items.content: Mona Khalil, who had refused to leave the beach she had spent years protecting, died from her injuries after the Israeli strike.

items.title: Israeli attack kills famed turtle sanctuary ecologist in Lebanon
items.standfirst: Mona Khalil led decades-long effort to protect nesting site for turtles near her home in south of the countryThe Lebanese marine activist Mona Khalil, who became a beloved figure in the country for a decades-long effort to protect a nesting site for turtles near her home, has died from injuries sustained in an Israeli strike.Khalil, 76, ran a sanctuary called the Orange House Project near the Mediterranean city of Tyre. She hosted volunteers in her house to clean and monitor a mile-long beach and welcomed tourists to stay and learn about conservation. Continue reading...
items.content: Mona Khalil led decades-long effort to protect nesting site for turtles near her home in south of the countryThe Lebanese marine activist Mona Khalil, who became a beloved figure in the country for a decades-long effort to protect a nesting site for turtles near her home, has died from injuries sustained in an Israeli strike.Khalil, 76, ran a sanctuary called the Orange House Project near the Mediterranean city of Tyre. She hosted volunteers in her house to clean and monitor a mile-long beach and welcomed tourists to stay and learn about conservation. Continue reading...

ai_summary: Mona Khalil dies from Israeli strike injuries

clusters.title: Scientists Invent a Way to Brew Espresso With Ultrasonic Waves—No Hot Water Required

items.title: Scientists Invent a Way to Brew Espresso With Ultrasonic Waves—No Hot Water Required
items.standfirst: Researchers have demonstrated they can make coffee comparable to conventional espresso using ultrasonic waves. Because the process doesn’t need hot water, it consumes 75 percent less energy.
items.content: Researchers have demonstrated they can make coffee comparable to conventional espresso using ultrasonic waves. Because the process doesn’t need hot water, it consumes 75 percent less energy.

items.title: Sound waves made this espresso—and could cut brewing energy use by 75%
items.standfirst: Ultrasonic espresso uses cold water and high-frequency sound waves to extract coffee with far less energy than conventional brewing. Early taste tests suggest it can match the traditional version. [theconversation.com]
items.content: Ultrasonic espresso uses cold water and high-frequency sound waves to extract coffee with far less energy than conventional brewing. Early taste tests suggest it can match the traditional version. [theconversation.com]

ai_summary: Researchers use ultrasonic waves for espresso

clusters.title: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery

items.title: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
items.standfirst: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.
items.content: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.

items.title: Global framework for reparatory justice adopted at landmark Ghana conference
items.standfirst: Call for formal apologies from countries that benefited from transatlantic trade slave included in 19-point roadmapMore than money: the logic of slavery reparationsA global framework for reparatory justice has been adopted at a conference in Ghana, as African and Caribbean leaders demanded formal apologies from countries that benefited from the transatlantic slave trade.Heads of state and government and other officials formally approved the strategy on Friday at a gathering in a hotel in the capital, Accra, which was the first major meeting since the adoption of the landmark United Nations (UN) resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...
items.content: Call for formal apologies from countries that benefited from transatlantic trade slave included in 19-point roadmapMore than money: the logic of slavery reparationsA global framework for reparatory justice has been adopted at a conference in Ghana, as African and Caribbean leaders demanded formal apologies from countries that benefited from the transatlantic slave trade.Heads of state and government and other officials formally approved the strategy on Friday at a gathering in a hotel in the capital, Accra, which was the first major meeting since the adoption of the landmark United Nations (UN) resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...

ai_summary: African and Caribbean nations seek apology
Formal apologies requested from benefiting countries
Debt relief and financial compensation sought
